title: External asset manifest (external_asset_v1)
description: What an external producer delivers alongside a 3D model so the game_iso_v1 pipeline can read it. See docs/external_asset_contract.md.

variant_id.description: unique per variant; becomes the output dir name, so two assets must not share it

archetype.description: logical family; selects the shared rig + animation library. Add a value here when a new archetype is introduced.

mesh.description: .glb (preferred) or .obj, relative to this manifest. For a rigged+animated variant this is the glb AFTER bake_anim_from_json.py has embedded the clips, OR pair a clip-less rigged glb with files.animation_clips below.

animation_clips.description: optional path to an anim_clips_v1 JSON (schema/animation_clips.schema.json). When present, bake_asset.py runs bake_anim_from_json.py to embed these clips into files.mesh (a rigged glb) before baking.

up.description: source up axis. HONORED for .obj (a Y-up obj without up:"y" bakes sideways). For .glb/.gltf the importer reads the file's own axis and this field is currently inert -- set it correctly anyway for portability.

forward.description: The model MUST face +X (= direction 0). This field is optional -- omit it and +x is assumed; if present it must be `+x`. It is declared-only and NOT applied by any baker yet, so a model built facing another way renders wrong. (Future: pipeline-applied rotation.)

rig.description: rig profile id (pipeline/schema/rig_profiles/<rig>.json). Omit for a static mesh -> procedural animation.

default_state.description: the state shown when nothing is playing (must be an `animations` key). Omit -> engine uses `idle` if present, else the lexicographically-first state.

region_source.description: how HIT regions are tagged. Only material_name bakes today; the others are documented extensions.

textures.description: named texture maps relative to this manifest. RENDER STATUS this iteration: only an embedded-glb base color renders (Workbench TEXTURE pass); base_color listed here, and normal/roughness/metallic, are RECORDED but NOT rendered. OBJ deliveries render a flat per-region color (no texture).

clip.description: glTF animation clip name embedded in the glb (defaults to the state key)

frames.description: sprite frames sampled per direction (the same value as the matching anim_clips_v1 clip's `frames`)

playback.description: loop wraps; once holds the last frame (engine ADR-044)

animations.description: state name -> clip sampling. The pipeline samples each named glb clip into `frames` poses x direction_count directions.

world_metrics.description: optional override; otherwise measured from the mesh AABB
